subtasks.description: Query TPC-H warehouse to identify top 10 customers by lifetime value in BUILDING market segment, including customer ID, name, total spend, and order count
Query TPC-H warehouse to extract detailed order patterns for the top 10 customers (order frequency, average order value, product categories, seasonal trends, payment behavior)

Reconcile customer lifetime value data with their order patterns; for each customer, synthesize a personalized retention narrative highlighting their purchase behavior, value contribution, and relationship strength

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks required: (1) Top 10 customers query is mid-tier (multi-table join on customer, orders, lineitem tables with aggregation and filtering on market segment); (2) Order pattern query is also mid-tier (requires window functions or CTEs to compute frequency, averages, and trends across orders); (3) Cross-recon for narrative generation is frontier-tier because it must synthesize two complex datasets, perform multi-signal analysis (LTV + order behavior patterns), and generate contextually personalized retention messaging—a task requiring sophisticated reasoning and inference. Cannot merge the two SQL queries into one because they require different aggregation levels: one groups by customer for LTV ranking, the other requires granular order-level details; keeping them separate allows efficient query optimization. The narrative synthesis cannot be done by either sql_gen alone—it requires the cross_recon agent's reconciliation and narrative capability.

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_spend, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS order_count FROM customer c JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10

output.raw_output:
{"sql": "SELECT
    c.c_custkey,
    c.c_name,
    c.c_mktsegment,
    c.c_acctbal,
    COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey) AS total_orders,
    SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS lifetime_value,
    AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_value,
    MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS first_order_date,
    MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS last_order_date,
    MAX(o.o_orderdate) - MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS customer_tenure_days,
    SUM(o.o_totalprice) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey), 0) AS avg_order_value_check,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderpriority = '1-URGENT' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS urgent_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderpriority = '2-HIGH' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS high_priority_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderpriority = '3-MEDIUM' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS medium_priority_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderpriority = '4-NOT SPECIFIED' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS not_specified_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderpriority = '5-LOW' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS low_priority_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) IN (1,2,3) THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS q1_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) IN (4,5,6) THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS q2_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) IN (7,8,9) THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS q3_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) IN (10,11,12) THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS q4_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderstatus = 'F' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS fulfilled_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderstatus = 'O' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS open_orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderstatus = 'P' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS pending_orders,
    SUM(li.l_quantity) AS total_quantity_purchased,
    AVG(li.l_discount) AS avg_discount_rate,
    SUM(li.l_extendedprice * (1 - li.l_discount)) AS net_
